entries.title: The Pentagon abruptly canceled deployment of an armored brigade to Europe, a major step toward shrinking the U.S. military posture in Europe that caught some military officials by surprise
Denise Powell Wins Tight Democratic Primary for Open House Seat in Nebraska
Trump administration health officials are walking a tightrope on hantavirus: trying to avoid public panic and a repeat of what they view as pandemic-era missteps
Kevin Warsh is officially the new Fed chair, the White House considers 250 pardons to mark America’s birthday, and Drake is back with a new album. Read more in today’s What’s News newsletter:
Vice President JD Vance delivered an ultimatum to all 50 states: Fully comply with antifraud statutes or run the risk of losing federal Medicaid funding
White House officials are weighing a plan for President Trump to issue 250 pardons as a way to mark the celebration of the nation’s 250th birthday, according to people familiar with the matter.
The Department of Homeland Security’s inspector general has launched a probe into the $38 billion warehouse-to-detention program championed by former Secretary Kristi Noem
Senior leaders in the White House concluded that FDA Commissioner Marty Makary was out of step with President Trump’s priorities
The Trump administration will designate David Venturella, a former private prison company executive and former career employee of ICE, as its next acting director
FDA Commissioner Marty Makary is leaving his role, President Trump told reporters Tuesday, after Makary had battled officials in the health department and the White House on a range of policy issues
PayPal agrees to waive $30 million in fees to settle Justice Department probe into minority-business funding program
A Nebraska state senator and a former communications executive were in a tight Democratic congressional primary early Wednesday for a House seat left open by retiring GOP Rep. Don Bacon
Louisiana Republican Rep. Julia Letlow is locked in a bitter primary that is testing the president’s power to shape MAGA’s next generation
An effort from Democrats to redraw maps in more states comes as two court rulings shatter the party’s optimism for beating Republicans in a gerrymandering battle
Republicans are playing defense in Ohio and a growing number of other red states, where Democrats are counting on voter backlash to power them to victory this fall in their fight to retake the Senate
Virginia’s highest court threw out Democrats’ effort to draw more blue-leaning seats ahead of the midterm elections, in a major win for Republicans
FBI Director Kash Patel fielded questions from lawmakers about allegations that he drinks excessively and other issues during a Senate hearing
The new battleship to be named after Trump will be nuclear-powered, a move expected to boost the cost and complexity of a project that was facing questions about its feasibility
President Trump privately complained to the acting attorney general about media leaks in the wake of the Iran war last month, prompting an aggressive push at the DOJ to pursue those investigations
President Trump escalated his public feud with Pope Leo XIV, accusing the pontiff of endangering Catholics by opposing U.S. military action against Iran
President Trump’s desire to end the Iran war is being put to the test after Tehran fired at American warships and violently disrupted a U.S. effort to revive shipping in the Strait of Hormuz
